Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 246 [House protection rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (1 column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[...] x

(o ii 1') [...] ... you pulverize [...] water.

o ii 2'2'

[...] ⸢A⸣ ta-sak₃

o ii 3'3'

[...] ⸢3⸣-šu₂ ana ŠA₃ ŠIDnu

(o ii 3') You recite [...] 3 times towards the centre.

o ii 4'4'

[... ki]-⸢iṣ⸣-ṣu-ru-tu₄ ina KA₂ AN.AŠ.AM₃

(o ii 4') You draw entwined [...] on the outer door and the breast of the foe and the enemy will be turned away.

o ii 5'5'

[ḪURir]-⸢ma⸣ GABA ḪUL u a-a-bi ⸢tur⸣-rat!(RI)

o ii 6'6'

[x x] dALAD!(KAL) u UŠ₂-MEŠ ana E₂ LU₂ NU TE-MEŠ

(o ii 6') [...], the šēdu-demon and plague will not approach a man's house.

o ii 7'7'

[x x] ⸢na₄⸣ka-gin₆-na dab-ba ⸢si⸣ x si sa₂-e

(o ii 7') [...]: 'Magnetite, directing the [...] horns,

o ii 8'8'

[x x]-⸢bi⸣-a gi₄-bi dx igi-ke₄

(o ii 8') '[...] ...,

o ii 9'9'

[x x a]-⸢a⸣-bi tur-ru ḫe₂-⸢em⸣-ta-e₃

(o ii 9') 'turning away the [...] of the enemy -- may it leave.

o ii 10'10'

[x x] ⸢ŋiškim⸣ ḫul ḫe₂-⸢em⸣-ta-e₃

(o ii 10') '[...] (and) evil sign -- may they leave.

o ii 11'11'

[x x niŋ₂]-⸢gig⸣ a₂-sag₃ ḫul ḫe₂-⸢em⸣-ta-e₃

(o ii 11') '[...], taboo (and) the evil asag-demon -- may they leave.

o ii 12'12'

[x] ⸢ḫul⸣ igi ḫul ka ḫul eme ḫul MIN<(ḫe₂-em-ta-e₃)>

(o ii 12') 'The evil [...], the evil eye, the evil mouth (and) the evil tongue -- may they leave.'

o ii 13'13'

[KA].⸢INIM⸣.MA ⸢GABA⸣ ḪUL [x] a-a-bi tur-ru

(o ii 13') Incantation for turning away the breast of the foe [...] the enemy.

o ii 14'14'

[DU₃.DU₃]-BI ŠU-BI.AŠ.AM₃

(o ii 14') Its ritual: ditto.

o ii 15'15'

[x x] GIR₃ ⸢ḪUL⸣ti₃ ina ⸢E₂-ia⸣-ma KU₅us  ; ⸢iq⸣-ta-bi

(o ii 15') [...] keeping away the foot of evil from my house he has said:

o ii 16'16'

[x x] ⸢še⸣-ga-me-en ⸢še-ga-me⸣-en

(o ii 16') [...]. 'I am the favourite, I am the favourite.

o ii 17'17'

[d]⸢utu⸣ še-⸢ga-me⸣-en ⸢di-kud še-ga⸣-me-en

(o ii 17') 'Utu, I am the favourite, judge, I am the favourite.

o ii 18'18'

[diŋir]-⸢ŋu₁₀⸣ še-⸢ga⸣-me-en ⸢diŋir⸣-re-⸢e⸣-ne

(o ii 18') 'My god, I am the favourite, gods, I am the favourite, man, I am the favourite.

o ii 19'19'

[še]-⸢ga-me-en⸣ lu₂ še-ga-me-⸢en⸣

o ii 20'20'

[x x še?]-⸢ga?-me?-en?⸣ zu? lu₂-lu₇lu

(o ii 20') ' [...], I am the favourite, wise one of humanity, [...] of mankind, I am the favourite.

o ii 21'21'

[x x nam?-lu₂?-lu₇?]⸢lu? še⸣-ga-me-en

o ii 22'22'

[x x ḫe₂-em]-⸢ta⸣-e₃ te EN₂

(o ii 22') '[...] -- may it leave.' Incantation formula.

o ii 23'23'

[... u₂IGI]-⸢lim u₂⸣ḫa-šu-u₂

(o ii 23') [...], imhur-līm-plant, thyme, [...] with hair from a [...] female kid; you sprinkle [...] of good [...].

o ii 24'24'

[...] ina SIG₂ munusAŠ₂.GAR₃

o ii 25'25'

[...] DU₁₀.GA ⸢SU₃⸣

o ii 26'26'

[...] ina GU₂-šu₂

(o ii 26') [...] on its neck [...] of evil is blocked.

o ii 27'27'

[... ḪUL]ti₃ KU₅at

o ii 28'28'

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ LU₂ KU₅si

(o ii 28') [...] blocking [...] a man's house:

o ii 29'29'

[...] x tab-nit

(o ii 29') [...] ... offspring of [...] ...

o ii 30'30'

[...] x UD

Column iii
(start of column missing)
o iii 1'o 1'

⸢TA?⸣ [x x] x x ⸢ZA₃⸣ u ⸢GUB₃ GUBzu⸣ u [x x]

(o iii 1') They (figures) stand ... [...] ... the right and left and [...].

o iii 2'2'

u₂-ma-ša₂ IL₂-šu₂-nu-tu₂ kušE.IB₂ GIR₂ ⸢ZABAR⸣

(o iii 2') You have them bear a grappling hook. You have them bear a belt for a bronze sword, belts for axes (and) ....

o iii 3'3'

šib-be-e GIN₂-MEŠ uruduKUR-ne₂-e IL₂-šu₂-nu-⸢tu₂⸣

o iii 4'4'

ziq-pa ina KUN₄ te-te-mir-šu₂-nu-tu₂

(o iii 4') You bury them upright at the threshold.

o iii 5'5'

ta-na-at-tu ta-dab-bu-ub-šu₂-nu-tu₂

(o iii 5') You speak praise to them:

o iii 6'6'

at-tu-nu ALAM IM PA₅ gišKIRI₆

(o iii 6') 'You, figures (made) from clay from the ditch in the orchard,

o iii 7'7'

ina A-MEŠ qid-da-ti ab-lul-ku-nu-ši

(o iii 7') 'I kneaded you in water (flowing) downstream.

o iii 8'8'

IGI dLAMMA ab-ni-ku-nu-ši

(o iii 8') 'I fashioned you before the protective goddess.

o iii 9'9'

u₂-kal-ku-nu-ši

(o iii 9') 'I hold you.

o iii 10'10'

ana GIR₃ ḪULti₃ ana E₂-ia₅ la e-re-bi

(o iii 10') 'For the foot of evil not to enter my house,

o iii 11'11'

ina KUN₄ u₂?-gam-mir-ku-nu-ši

(o iii 11') 'I assembled you in your entirety at the threshold.

o iii 12'12'

d⸢ALAD⸣ LUGAL nam-ru dUTU u₂-tam-me-ku-nu-ši

(o iii 12') 'I adjure you by the protective god (and) the radiant king, Šamaš.'

o iii 13'13'

⸢GIR₃⸣ ḪULti₃ ina E₂-ia₅ pur-ru-sa

(o iii 13') To keep away the foot of evil from my house, ... [...] ... and keep away the foot of evil, you approach the foot of evil, the šēdu-demon, the rābiṣu-demon (and) the evil god to the man's house, as well as the figures bearing the plague.

o iii 14'14'

x [x] x ḪA DA NIM-ma GIR₃ ḪULti₃ pur-ru-sa

o iii 15'15'

⸢GIR₃⸣ ḪUL⸢ti₃⸣ dALAD MAŠKIM DINGIR ḪUL ana E₂ LU₂

o iii 16'16'

tu-ṭaḫ-ḫa-am-ma NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ NU-MEŠ na-šu-tu₂

o iii 17'17'

ina KUN₄ KA₂ BAR.RA KEŠ₂ tara-kas₂ munusAŠ₂.GAR₃

(o iii 17') You set out a ritual assemblage at the threshold of the outer door. You slaughter a virgin female kid. You mix the blood, which you sink into a bowl, with water from the well in Marduk's temple and milk, and you sprinkle that house.

o iii 18'18'

GIŠ₃.NU.ZU [KU₅]is UŠ₂-MEŠ ša₂ ana kal-li

o iii 19'19'

ta-maḫ-⸢ḫa⸣-ḫu ina A PU₂ ša₂ E₂ dAMAR.UTU

o iii 20'20'

u GA ḪI.ḪI-ma E₂ BI SU₃

o iii 21'21'

E₂ BI ina gišAN.NA.GIŠIMMAR ša₂ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ tu-še-šer

(o iii 21') You sweep that house with a date-palm spadix from the (tree's) northern side.

o iii 22'22'

šu-šu-rat E₂ ana ID₂ ŠUBdi

(o iii 22') You throw the sweepings from the house into a watercourse.

o iii 23'23'

GIR₂ ZABAR ša₂ 10 GIN₂ KI.LA₂-BI TIqe₂-ma

(o iii 23') You take a bronze sword whose weight is 10 shekels, and you release (it) at the threshold of the outer door, and you drive (it) in deep towards the house.

o iii 24'24'

ina KUN₄ KA₂ AN.AŠ.AM₃ BAR-ma ana E₂ tu-šap-<pal>

o iii 25'25'

⸢GAR?⸣an ši-ik-ru ana KI.TAnu ⸢za⸣-aq-pa-an

(o iii 25') You place (it thus). You place the hilt below (and) the tip of evil above.

o iii 26'26'

ḪULti₃ ana AN.TAnu GARan

o iii 27'27'

ta-kam-mis-ma UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

(o iii 27') You kneel and you speak as follows:

o iii 28'28'

GIR₂ ZABAR ina ANe u KIti₃ x x ⸢TA⸣-ma

(o iii 28') 'Bronze sword ... in heaven and earth, and diʾu-illness (and) plague [...] in the man's house.'

o iii 29'29'

di-ḫu-um NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ ina E₂ ⸢LU₂⸣ [...]

o iii 30'30'

3-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma is!(IŠ-)ḫu-⸢ra?⸣ [...]

(o iii 30') You say (this) 3 times and [...] 'He searched'.

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

KUN₄? x [...]

(r i 1) The threshold ... you confine [...]. ... [...].

r i 22

te-si-ir ⸢KI? DU KA⸣ x MA? x [...]

r i 33

⸢EN₂?⸣ ḫu-u₂ ḫu-ba ḫu-ḫu is-ḫu-ra is-⸢ḫu⸣-[ra]

(r i 3) Incantation. 'Hu, huba, huhu, he searched, he searched.

r i 44

as-ḫu-ra a-ta-mar-ka as-ḫu-rak-ka eš-te-e-ka

(r i 4) 'I searched, I have seen you, I searched for you, I have sought you.

r i 55

ana la ḫa-ba-li-ia ana la ša₂-ga-ši-ia₂

(r i 5) 'For me not to be wronged, for me not to be killed,

r i 66

aš-šum GIM UR.MAḪ ta-qu-ur-tu₂ la ti-šu-u

(r i 6) 'because like a lion you do not have a ...,

r i 77

aš-šum GIM UR.BAR.RA ta-kal-tu₂ la TUKUu₂

(r i 7) 'because like a wolf you do not get a ...,

r i 88

šam-ru gišTUKUL-MEŠ-ka as-sa-pan-ka es-si-ir-ki

(r i 8) 'I have overwhelmed your furious weapons (and) I confine you.'

r i 99

EN₂ an-ni-tu₂ 3-šu₂ ana UGU KUN₄ ŠID-ma

(r i 9) You recite this incantation 3 times over the threshold and the foot of evil, diʾu-illness and plague will not approach the man's house.

r i 1010

GIR₃ ḪULti₃ di-ḫu u NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ ana E₂ LU₂ NU TE-⸢MEŠ⸣

r i 1111

GIR₃ ḪULti₃ E₃-ma dALAD SIG₅ u dLAMMA

(r i 11) The foot of evil will go out and the good šēdu-demon and the favourable protective goddess will enter the man's house. The god will be at peace with the man.

r i 1212

ŠE.GA ana E₂ LU₂ KU₄ DINGIR KI LU₂ i-sal-lim

r i 1313

ana E₂ LU₂ di-ḫu GIG u NAM.UŠ₂-MEŠ la₃ TEe

(r i 13) (For) diʾu-illness, illness and plague not to approach a man's house:

r i 1414

NUMUN u₂SAR NUMUN u₂IN.NU.UŠ NUMUN EME.⸢UR⸣.[GI₇]

(r i 14) seeds of greenery, seeds of maštakal-plant, seeds of hound's tongue, seeds of azallû-plant, seeds of šurdunû-plant, seeds of thorn, seeds of anameru-plant -- [...] these 7 plants together.

r i 1515

NUMUN u₂A.ZAL.LA₂ NUMUN u₂SI.SA₂ NUMUN u₂pu-[qut-ti]

r i 1616

NUMUN u₂a-na-mi-ru 7 U₂-ḪI.A an-nu-tu₂ ⸢1⸣[niš x]

r i 1717

ina LAL₃ KUR.RA ⸢u₂⸣-paṭ gišKIŠI₁₆ ḪI.ḪI ⸢3⸣-[šu₂]

(r i 17) You mix (them) with honey from the mountain (and) exudation from camelthorn. You divide (them) 3 times and you bury (them) at the threshold of the outer door, on the right [...], and illness, diʾu-illness, anxiety and plague [...] to a man [...].

r i 1818

ta-za-az-ma ina KUN₄ KA₂ BAR.RA ⸢ZA₃⸣ [x x]

r i 1919

te-⸢te-mir⸣-ma GIG di-ḫu ⸢di⸣-[lip-tu₂]

r i 2020

u NAM.UŠ₂-⸢MEŠ⸣ ana ⸢LU₂⸣ [...]

r i 2121

ana ⸢NA⸣ [...]

(r i 21) [...] to a man [...]

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[... ta?]-paṭ-tar?

(r ii 1) You release [...].

r ii 22

[...] ⸢ta?⸣-paṭ-tar?

(r ii 2) You release [...].

r ii 33

[...] ⸢šu⸣-lum-šu₂

(r ii 3) [...] his health [...] you open [...] of the threshold and [...] in the foundations of the house [...] at the head of the bed, you bury [...] of the outer [...].

r ii 44

[...] ⸢KUN₄⸣ BAD-ma

r ii 55

[...] ina SUḪUŠ E₂

r ii 66

[...] ina SAG gišNU₂

r ii 77

[... AN.AŠ].⸢AM₃⸣ te-te-mir

r ii 88

[...] U₂ ša₂ kap-pa GARnu

(r ii 8) [...] ... that are provided with wings, [...] head of the bed, bodies [...] you place their [...] towards the outside and -- ditto.

r ii 99

[...] ⸢SAG⸣ gišNU₂ pag-ru-u₂

r ii 1010

[...]-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ana KA₂-a-ni GAR-ma MIN

r ii 1111

[...] ša₂ MUNUS ša₂ gišGIŠIMMAR na-šu-u₂

(r ii 11) [...] that are bearing the ... of the date palm, [...] that are lying around by the chair, [...] their faces towards the outside, you bury [...].

r ii 1212

[...] ša₂? KI? gišGU.ZA saḫ?-ru-u₂

r ii 1313

[...] IGI-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ana KA₂-a-ni

r ii 1414

[...] te-te-mir

r ii 1515

[...] x GARan giURI₃.GAL na-šu-u₂

(r ii 15) You place [...]. ... Bearing a standard, [...] lying around [...] front of the chair -- ditto.

r ii 1616

[...] ⸢IGI⸣ gišGU.ZA ⸢saḫ?⸣-ru-u₂ MIN?

r ii 1717

[...] ⸢e₂⸣pa-pa-⸢ḫi⸣ [x] x RI GIŠ

(r ii 17) [...] of the cella [...] ..., [...] ... of the ēru-tree, [...] of the cella -- ditto.

r ii 1818

[...] x ḪA ša₂ gišMA.NU

r ii 1919

[... e₂?pa?]-pa?-ḫi? MIN

r ii 2020

[...] ⸢KA₂⸣ E₂ DINGIR-šu₂

(r ii 20) [...] gate of the temple of his god [...] ...
